summary:
The first paranasal sinus to begin development is the maxillary sinus, initiating at around the 10th week of gestation due to fusion of the ethmoid infundibulum with surrounding tissue. It precedes other sinuses like frontal or sphenoid, both of which mature much later in infancy or childhood. 

Final Answer: A

The maxillary sinus is the first paranasal sinus to initiate development, occurring as early as the 10th week of gestation through the fusion of the ethmoid infundibulum into a primitive cavity. Unlike posterior sinuses such as frontal and sphenoid—which appear significantly later—the maxillary sinus starts forming before birth and thus emerges earliest clinically among all paranasal sinuses.  

Final Answer: A
